mechanism_narrative: A positive 3-month trend in retail sales signals near-term revenue growth and improving household demand, raising expected corporate cash flows and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if higher 3-month RSAFS growth does not produce higher 20-day S&P 500 returns than lower RSAFS growth in walk-forward tests, or if Rank IC is below 0.03 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor income and recession risk, reducing expected consumption and earnings; when unemployment momentum turns upward, S&P 500 forward returns are negative over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered instances have positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of out-of-sample cases, or if triggered returns are not below unconditional returns at p <= 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate crossing above its prior 12-month level signals monetary tightening, lifting discount rates and compressing equity valuation multiples; this produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS crosses above its trailing 12-month maximum
failure_condition: Reject if S&P 500 60-day forward returns following FEDFUNDS upside crossings are not lower than non-crossing periods, or if the estimated return spread is positive in two consecutive 3-year walk-forward windows.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll growth combined with a falling unemployment rate confirms broad labor-market healing, increasing wage income and reducing recession probability; this joint improvement produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month change is positive and UNRATE falls over the same 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if the joint PAYEMS-up and UNRATE-down condition does not outperform cases where only one labor series improves, or if 60-day triggered returns are positive in less than 50% of out-of-sample cases.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained contraction in industrial production signals weakening output, inventory correction, and pressure on operating leverage; when production falls sharply, S&P 500 forward returns are negative over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day S&P 500 returns after three consecutive INDPRO declines are not below unconditional returns, or if the hit rate for negative returns is below 55% in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: A housing-starts recovery signals improving credit availability, construction employment, and durable-goods demand; when housing starts break above their recent trend, S&P 500 forward returns are positive over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST crosses above its trailing 6-month moving average from below
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST upside crossings do not produce higher 60-day S&P 500 returns than downside crossings, or if the out-of-sample return spread is negative over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation acceleration while the policy rate remains near zero raises expected policy normalization risk without providing current income support; this joint condition produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month growth accelerates and FEDFUNDS is below 0.25
failure_condition: Reject if the CPIAUCSL-acceleration and low-FEDFUNDS interaction does not underperform low-FEDFUNDS periods without inflation acceleration, or if 20-day return differences have p > 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Positive real GDP growth confirms broad demand expansion and supports aggregate earnings expectations; higher quarterly GDPC1 growth produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days after the data are observable.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth crosses above zero
failure_condition: Reject if positive GDPC1 growth crossings are not followed by higher 5-day S&P 500 returns than negative growth readings, or if the directional accuracy is below 52% in out-of-sample tests.
